title: Kohleria trianae
intro: Kohleria trianae, also known as the "Pride of Peru" or "Peruvian Pansy," is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Gesneriaceae, characterized by its vibrant colors and delicate appearance. Traditionally, Kohleria trianae is used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Kohleria trianae include iridoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Kohleria trianae is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into ointments and salves to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Kohleria trianae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant is a part of the Gesneriaceae family, which includes approximately 130 genera of flowering plants. Within the Gesneriaceae family, Kohleria trianae is part of the genus Kohleria, which comprises around 25 species. The Gesneriaceae family is further classified under the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is a part of the larger clade called Asterids in the angiosperm classification.

benefits: Kohleria trianae is known to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues.
